content.text: Few-Shot from Feedback

Few-shot prompting places worked examples directly in the prompt before the live request. When those examples are sourced from real human corrections, preference winners, and high-rated outputs collected in production, they carry the exact quality signal the model needs. This approach requires no model weights update, deploys in minutes, and improves output quality on the dimensions the examples cover. The challenge is selection: injecting irrelevant or stale examples wastes context and can confuse the model.

attrs.alt: Few-shot selection pipeline from feedback sources through ranker to assembled prompt

content.text: Example Record Schema

Every candidate example must store the prompt context alongside the ideal output so the ranker can measure similarity to the live request. Include a quality score derived from the annotation that produced the example, and a dimension tag so examples are only injected on matching request types.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import StrEnum
import uuid, time, json, hashlib


class ExampleSource(StrEnum):
    CORRECTION        = "correction"        # human edited a bad output
    PREFERENCE_WINNER = "preference_winner" # chosen side of a comparison pair
    HIGH_RATED        = "high_rated"        # ordinal score >= 4
    MANUAL            = "manual"            # authored by an expert directly


@dataclass
class FewShotExample:
    example_id: str
    source: ExampleSource
    messages: list[dict]   # conversation turns up to (not including) the ideal output
    ideal_output: str
    dimension: str | None  # "accuracy" | "helpfulness" | "safety" | None = general
    quality_score: float   # 0.0–1.0; drives ranking
    created_at: float
    reviewed: bool = False # True once a human has approved this as a canonical example

    @staticmethod
    def from_correction(
        messages: list[dict],
        corrected_output: str,
        dimension: str | None = None,
    ) -> "FewShotExample":
        return FewShotExample(
            example_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            source=ExampleSource.CORRECTION,
            messages=messages,
            ideal_output=corrected_output,
            dimension=dimension,
            quality_score=0.90,   # corrections carry high signal
            created_at=time.time(),
        )

    @staticmethod
    def from_preference_winner(
        messages: list[dict],
        chosen_output: str,
        confidence: int,        # 1-3 from the comparison annotation
        dimension: str | None = None,
    ) -> "FewShotExample":
        # Map confidence 1→0.70, 2→0.80, 3→0.92
        score = 0.60 + confidence * 0.10 + (0.02 if confidence == 3 else 0)
        return FewShotExample(
            example_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            source=ExampleSource.PREFERENCE_WINNER,
            messages=messages,
            ideal_output=chosen_output,
            dimension=dimension,
            quality_score=score,
            created_at=time.time(),
        )

    def user_text(self) -> str:
        """Return the last user message text for similarity computation."""
        for m in reversed(self.messages):
            if m["role"] == "user":
                return m["content"] if isinstance(m["content"], str) else ""
        return ""
Similarity-Based Ranking

Inject examples that are topically close to the live request. At small scale, SequenceMatcher or BM25 over the user text is sufficient. At larger scale, embed both the live request and each candidate example, then rank by cosine similarity. Always combine similarity with quality score and recency to avoid over-representing one topic.

from difflib import SequenceMatcher
import math


QUALITY_WEIGHT   = 0.35
SIMILARITY_WEIGHT = 0.50
RECENCY_WEIGHT   = 0.15
QUALITY_THRESHOLD = 0.75   # drop examples below this score
MAX_EXAMPLES      = 5
MAX_EXAMPLE_TOKENS = 400   # per example; keep total prompt lean


def similarity(text_a: str, text_b: str) -> float:
    """Fast token-overlap similarity. Replace with cosine if embeddings available."""
    return SequenceMatcher(None, text_a.lower(), text_b.lower()).ratio()


def recency_score(created_at: float, half_life_days: float = 30.0) -> float:
    """Exponential decay: examples fade to 0.5 quality weight after half_life_days."""
    age_days = (time.time() - created_at) / 86400
    return math.exp(-math.log(2) * age_days / half_life_days)


def rank_examples(
    pool: list[FewShotExample],
    live_user_text: str,
    dimension: str | None,
    max_examples: int = MAX_EXAMPLES,
) -> list[FewShotExample]:
    """
    Return up to max_examples examples sorted by composite score.
    Filters by dimension and quality threshold first.
    """
    candidates = [
        ex for ex in pool
        if ex.quality_score >= QUALITY_THRESHOLD
        and (dimension is None or ex.dimension is None or ex.dimension == dimension)
    ]

    scored = []
    for ex in candidates:
        sim   = similarity(live_user_text, ex.user_text())
        rec   = recency_score(ex.created_at)
        score = (
            SIMILARITY_WEIGHT * sim +
            QUALITY_WEIGHT    * ex.quality_score +
            RECENCY_WEIGHT    * rec
        )
        scored.append((score, ex))

    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
    return [ex for _, ex in scored[:max_examples]]
Assembling the Prompt

Interleave examples as alternating user/assistant turns before the live request. Place them after the system prompt and before any tool definitions. Keep examples in quality-descending order — the model attends most strongly to the last example before the live turn, so put the highest-quality example last.

def build_prompt_with_few_shot(
    system: str,
    live_messages: list[dict],
    examples: list[FewShotExample],
    max_tokens_per_example: int = MAX_EXAMPLE_TOKENS,
) -> tuple[str, list[dict]]:
    """
    Return (system_prompt, messages_list) ready for the Claude API.
    Examples are inserted between the system prompt and live messages.
    """
    few_shot_turns: list[dict] = []

    # Quality-ascending order → best example is closest to live request
    ordered = sorted(examples, key=lambda e: e.quality_score)

    for ex in ordered:
        # Truncate ideal output to token budget
        output = ex.ideal_output
        if len(output) > max_tokens_per_example * 4:   # rough char proxy
            output = output[: max_tokens_per_example * 4] + " [...]"

        # Use only the last user message from the example
        user_msg = next(
            (m for m in reversed(ex.messages) if m["role"] == "user"), None
        )
        if user_msg is None:
            continue

        few_shot_turns.append({"role": "user",      "content": user_msg["content"]})
        few_shot_turns.append({"role": "assistant",  "content": output})

    full_messages = few_shot_turns + live_messages
    return system, full_messages


import anthropic

async def run_with_few_shot(
    system: str,
    live_messages: list[dict],
    pool: list[FewShotExample],
    dimension: str | None = None,
    model: str = "claude-opus-4-8",
) -> str:
    live_user_text = next(
        (m["content"] for m in reversed(live_messages) if m["role"] == "user"), ""
    )
    examples = rank_examples(pool, live_user_text, dimension)
    system_prompt, messages = build_prompt_with_few_shot(system, live_messages, examples)

    client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()
    resp = await client.messages.create(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=1024,
        system=system_prompt,
        messages=messages,
    )
    return resp.content[0].text
Storing and Maintaining the Example Pool

The pool is a small, curated database — not a bulk dump of all annotated outputs. Cap it at a few hundred examples per dimension and prune low-quality or stale entries periodically. Require a human review flag on examples sourced from automated preference selection before they enter the live pool.

import sqlite3

def init_example_pool(path: str = "few_shot_pool.db") -> sqlite3.Connection:
    conn = sqlite3.connect(path)
    conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS examples (
            example_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
            source        TEXT NOT NULL,
            messages      TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON
            ideal_output  TEXT NOT NULL,
            dimension     TEXT,
            quality_score REAL NOT NULL,
            created_at    REAL NOT NULL,
            reviewed      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
        )
    """)
    conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dim ON examples (dimension, quality_score DESC)")
    conn.commit()
    return conn


def add_example(conn: sqlite3.Connection, ex: FewShotExample) -> None:
    conn.execute(
        "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO examples VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
        (ex.example_id, ex.source.value, json.dumps(ex.messages),
         ex.ideal_output, ex.dimension, ex.quality_score,
         ex.created_at, int(ex.reviewed)),
    )
    conn.commit()


def load_pool(
    conn: sqlite3.Connection,
    dimension: str | None = None,
    reviewed_only: bool = True,
    limit: int = 300,
) -> list[FewShotExample]:
    query = "SELECT * FROM examples WHERE 1=1"
    params: list = []
    if dimension:
        query += " AND (dimension = ? OR dimension IS NULL)"
        params.append(dimension)
    if reviewed_only:
        query += " AND reviewed = 1"
    query += " ORDER BY quality_score DESC LIMIT ?"
    params.append(limit)

    rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
    return [
        FewShotExample(
            example_id=r[0], source=ExampleSource(r[1]),
            messages=json.loads(r[2]), ideal_output=r[3],
            dimension=r[4], quality_score=r[5],
            created_at=r[6], reviewed=bool(r[7]),
        )
        for r in rows
    ]


def prune_stale(conn: sqlite3.Connection, max_age_days: int = 90) -> int:
    import time
    cutoff = time.time() - max_age_days * 86400
    result = conn.execute(
        "DELETE FROM examples WHERE created_at < ? AND quality_score < 0.85",
        (cutoff,),
    )
    conn.commit()
    return result.rowcount
Evaluating Example Effectiveness

Measure whether injecting examples actually raises quality on a held-out eval set. Run the same prompts with and without the few-shot block and compare pass rates. Track per-dimension lift. If an example set delivers less than two percentage points of lift, audit whether the examples are semantically close enough to the test prompts.

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

def eval_few_shot_lift(
    system: str,
    eval_cases: list[dict],   # [{"messages": [...], "expected": str}]
    pool: list[FewShotExample],
    model: str = "claude-opus-4-8",
    judge_model: str = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
) -> dict:
    """
    Compare pass rate with and without few-shot injection.
    'expected' is not used directly — a judge scores each output instead.
    """
    def judge(output: str, expected: str) -> bool:
        resp = client.messages.create(
            model=judge_model,
            max_tokens=8,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content":
                f"Is this response correct and helpful? Yes or no.\n\n{output}"}],
        )
        return "yes" in resp.content[0].text.lower()

    baseline_pass, few_shot_pass = 0, 0

    for case in eval_cases:
        live_msgs = case["messages"]
        expected  = case.get("expected", "")

        # Baseline: no few-shot
        resp_base = client.messages.create(
            model=model, max_tokens=512, system=system, messages=live_msgs,
        )
        if judge(resp_base.content[0].text, expected):
            baseline_pass += 1

        # With few-shot
        user_text = next(
            (m["content"] for m in reversed(live_msgs) if m["role"] == "user"), ""
        )
        examples = rank_examples(pool, user_text, dimension=None)
        _, few_shot_msgs = build_prompt_with_few_shot(system, live_msgs, examples)
        resp_fs = client.messages.create(
            model=model, max_tokens=512, system=system, messages=few_shot_msgs,
        )
        if judge(resp_fs.content[0].text, expected):
            few_shot_pass += 1

    n = len(eval_cases)
    return {
        "baseline_pass_rate": round(baseline_pass / n, 3),
        "few_shot_pass_rate": round(few_shot_pass / n, 3),
        "lift": round((few_shot_pass - baseline_pass) / n, 3),
        "n": n,
    }
Common Pitfalls
Too many examples: more than 5-6 examples eat context budget and push the live request toward the end of the window where attention is weaker. Keep the block under 2000 tokens.
Irrelevant examples: injecting examples from a different domain can anchor the model to the wrong style. Always filter by dimension and similarity score.
Stale examples: corrections from six months ago may encode outdated product behaviour. Set a quality_score decay and prune examples older than 90 days that fall below 0.85.
Skipping review: automated selection from preference winners can include low-quality pairs. Require a reviewed=True flag before an example enters the live pool.
Repeating the same example: if the pool is small, the same example may appear in nearly every prompt. Monitor per-example usage count and cap it.
Summary

Few-shot prompting from feedback turns collected annotations into a real-time quality lever. Build a curated pool of FewShotExample records sourced from human corrections, preference winners, and high-rated outputs. Rank candidates by a composite score (similarity × 0.50 + quality × 0.35 + recency × 0.15) and inject the top 4–5 before the live request in quality-ascending order. Maintain the pool by requiring human review before entries go live and pruning stale low-quality examples quarterly. Measure actual lift with a held-out eval set comparing pass rates with and without the few-shot block.
